hero_subtitle: SFTY connects and normalizes screening provider outputs to streamline sanctions and adverse media reviews while preserving audit-ready evidence for digital asset compliance teams.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Manual sanctions screening creates compliance gaps
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle when sanctions and adverse media results come from multiple disconnected providers. This fragmentation slows alert triage, weakens audit evidence, and complicates case management workflows for regulated digital asset businesses.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY aggregates sanctions and adverse media results from multiple providers and routes alerts into a unified case management workflow with full decision logging and audit-ready evidence.
workflow_title: From screening results to compliance decisions
workflow_description: SFTY consolidates sanctions and adverse media screening outputs, applies rules, routes reviews, opens cases, preserves evidence and records decisions within one workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another dashboard. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects sanctions and adverse media screening outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence without replacing existing screening providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY gives VASPs a secure orchestration layer that preserves audit evidence and supports governance for sanctions screening and bank due diligence workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for sanctions screening workflows?
faq_1_answer: SFTY acts as a compliance control layer that connects sanctions and adverse media screening outputs into structured workflows, decisions, case management, and reporting for VASPs and digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage screening alerts and cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY normalizes screening provider outputs, applies internal policies, routes alerts for review, creates cases, logs decisions and links evidence into one controlled compliance workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and supporting evidence with traceable audit records to support compliance review and regulatory inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing screening providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY connects multiple existing screening providers without disruption, aggregating their outputs to deliver a connected compliance workflow and supporting rule enforcement across jurisdictions.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace our current screening tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace screening tools but works above them, turning fragmented outputs into structured, auditable workflows, decision records and evidence packages.
final_title: Connect sanctions screening into compliance workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY integrates sanctions and adverse media provider outputs into structured cases, decision logs and audit-ready evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
